chunks.text: In Japanese settlement, old legends remain.
Fox, tanuki, kappa, tengu.
Demons of hell.
The stories spilled out of these are heard in the ears from childhood.
These youkai are not mere products of imagination.
They were a roll of the wisdom of our ancestors, used to explain natural phenomena.
The fox is said to be the messenger of the god Inari.
Like a servant of the shrine, fox statues guard the entrance.
The kappa is said to live near rivers.
It has a pit on its head filled with water.
Adults would urge children, " Be careful at rivers.
The tengu lives in the throat parts of mountains and has a red face and a long nose.
It would make ascetics bend.
These stories taught children awe and fear of nature.
The emphasis that humans cannot rule nature.
Today, youkai have become characters in manga and anime.
" Pokemon " and " Spirited Away " bear strong influence from Japanese youkai culture.
As traditional regime is being lost through urbanization, youkai continue to live in new forms.
They are a source for the imagination of maker.
Even in the age of rationalism, whereas everything can be explained
we have not lost our sense of the invisible.
At night, walking a mountain path, one suddenly turns and feels something is there.
That sense is the core of Japanese youkai culture.
